mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned near the zero lower bound (currently 0.19%) compresses the equity risk premium discount rate on a continuous basis, mechanically supporting forward S&P 500 returns as long as policy remains accommodative, independent of any trigger threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE remains above 9.0% but shows a month-over-month decline, markets interpret the crossing as an inflection from labor-market destruction toward recovery, pricing in improved future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9.0% and month-over-month change turns negative for the first time in 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 60d returns not statistically distinguishable from non-triggered baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or hit rate of positive returns falls below 50% across at least two distinct triggering episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicates a broad-based industrial and employment expansion that is not visible from either series alone; this joint confirmation signal predicts positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0 AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods produce forward 20d returns with Rank IC below 0.03, or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) predicts positive forward S&P 500 returns specifically during a post-recession recovery regime characterized by elevated but improving UNRATE, because consumer spending resumption signals demand-side recovery that is discounted more strongly when the starting point is depressed; the same RSAFS growth carries weaker predictive power in normal-cycle regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% with FEDFUNDS at 0.19% consistent with post-recession recovery regime
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with elevated unemployment above 8%
failure_condition: RSAFS growth-to-forward-return correlation in the elevated-UNRATE regime is not significantly higher than in low-UNRATE regimes (regime interaction coefficient not significant at 10% level), or Rank IC < 0.03 within the regime subsample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned below 0.25% while CPIAUCSL shows subdued year-over-year growth, markets interpret this combination as evidence of disinflationary or deflationary risk that overrides the stimulative effect of low rates, producing a short-horizon negative reaction in S&P 500 returns as growth expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show forward 5d returns that are positive on average or not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one distinct historical episode.

mechanism_narrative: The continuous month-over-month rate of change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) acts as an always-on leading indicator of aggregate income and consumption, with positive payroll growth trends associated with positive forward S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month regardless of level or regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or sign concordance between PAYEMS trend and return direction falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a synchronized recovery across both durable-goods investment and consumer spending channels that neither series alone captures, and this combined demand-side confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average rises AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 610 thousand units near cycle lows suggests room for acceleration
failure_condition: Joint HOUST/RSAFS acceleration periods fail to produce forward 60d returns exceeding the unconditional sample mean at 10% significance, or the interaction term is dominated by RSAFS alone in decomposition tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month annualized growth rate of industrial production (INDPRO) crosses from negative to positive, it marks an inflection from contraction to expansion in the industrial supply chain, and this crossing event is followed by positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets re-rate cyclical earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate crosses above 0% from a negative value
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following the crossing event are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) across at least three historical crossing episodes, or the crossing signal shows negative Rank IC in walk-forward validation.
